intent.one_sentence: A sunlit desert ruin arranged as a material gallery for stone, sand, metal, ceramic, and glass.
intent.art_goal: Show strong sun, long shadows, sand buildup, material breakup, and non-procedural prop staging.

foreground_midground_background.foreground: Broken stone plinth edge with sand buildup.
foreground_midground_background.midground: Relic objects on grounded bases with metal and glass accents.
foreground_midground_background.background: Ruined arches and bright sky haze.

lighting.key: Hard warm sun from upper right.
lighting.fill: Cool sky bounce into shadow sides.
lighting.rim: Low amber bounce from sand and relic metals.
lighting.fog: Dry dust haze in the background.

cameras.role: Ruins, relic plinths, sand, and shadow pattern.
cameras.art_reason: Wider low view keeps the relic readable while giving scanned stones, branch debris, sand ramps, and ruin walls enough scale to feel authored.

cameras.role: Stone edge wear, sand, and oxidized metal response.
cameras.art_reason: Close enough to judge material palette without flattening the ruin.
Frames relics through architecture instead of isolated props.

material_palette.description: Light stone with chipped edges and darker grime in cracks.
Dry sand buildup around object bases.
Warm oxidized metal, not mirror chrome.
Small colored glass accent catching sun highlights.

assets.art_reason: Grounded central base gives all relics a coherent stage.
Round prop is deliberately scaled evenly and seated on a base.
Sand wraps the base and prevents the scene from reading as clean blocks.
Scanned stones interrupt the primitive plinth silhouette and give sand contact points.
Dry branch debris adds non-blocky foreground silhouette and scale context.

authored_groups.art_reason: Central object, base, sand, and accents are one staged material vignette.
Arches create depth and shadow direction around the relics.
Sand and grime connect objects to ground without random scatter.

seeded_random_groups.art_reason: Small chips reinforce wear; they do not define the composition.
